data.title: China Is Setting Rules to Limit Nvidia's H200 Chip Purchases
data.description:
Beijing is reportedly drafting rules to cap imports of advanced artificial intelligence (AI) chips from Nvidia (NVDA) and other foreign players, according to Nikkei Asia. Regulators are working on defining how many chips a company can buy, alongside strict requirements of domestic chip purchases.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Through these regulations, Beijing is striving to balance its push for domestic chip innovation with the needs of Chinese tech giants for access to top-tier hardware. Yesterday, NVDA shares dropped 1.4% on news that China instructed domestic firms to avoid H200 purchases.

Beijing to Regulate Import Volumes

The regulations would cap total volumes of cutting-edge AI chips local firms can buy, allowing limited Nvidia sales instead of an outright ban. Beijing could grant its first approvals for limited H200 chip purchases by month's end, though companies must justify their needs. Officials will likely decide which firms receive how many chips, though details remain unfinalized.

The Chinese government is reportedly holding regular meetings with top tech firms to understand their use of foreign chips, asking for explanations on why domestic alternatives cannot replace them, particularly for less intensive tasks like inferencing, or deploying trained AI models. Officials have also begun soliciting Chinese tech firms' views on demand for Nvidia's next-gen Blackwell chips, which Washington has yet to approve for export.

Despite Washington's recent reversal allowing H200 sales, Beijing has withheld import approvals to protect local chipmakers like Huawei, Horizon Robotics, Cambricon, and emerging players such as Moore Threads, MetaX, Biren Technology, and Suiyuan Technology.

Nvidia Caught in the Crosshairs

The U.S. government recently approved the sale of Nvidia's H200 GPUs (graphics processing units) to China, with conditions such as revenue-sharing and limited share of shipments relative to domestic sales.

Chinese tech giants like Alibaba (BABA), Tencent (TCEHY), and ByteDance continue to prioritize Nvidia's AI server racks for their unmatched computing power, which fuels ambitions in training, inference, and simulations. Against this backdrop, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang noted this month that while the H200 remains competitive in China for now, "it won't be competitive forever." He added that the company will eventually have to roll out its Blackwell and Rubin chips in China on time, subject to approval. Washington's abrupt policy shift marks a stark reversal from its prior efforts to bar China from advanced technologies.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Analysts remain highly optimistic about Nvidia's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 40 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $264.30 implies 44.3% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, NVDA shares have gained 34.4%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Rocket Lab (RKLB) Stock Surged 6% to a Record High
data.description:
Shares of space launch company Rocket Lab USA (RKLB) jumped more than 6% on Wednesday, as satellite-related stocks extended their rally amid rising geopolitical tensions. In addition, Cantor Fitzgerald five-star analyst Andres Sheppard reiterated his Buy rating on RKLB, calling Rocket Lab the top alternative to Elon Musk's SpaceX. RKLB shares reached a new high of $91.80, but fell 2% in after-hours trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Overall, the satellite sector is picking up steam as global tensions rise, driven by recent White House moves and sharp rhetoric around Venezuela, Iran, and even Denmark, tied to President Trump's renewed focus on Greenland. Other players in the sector, including Planet Labs (PL) and EchoStar (SATS), also climbed roughly 6% on Wednesday.

Cantor Fitzgerald Stays Bullish on RKLB

Sheppard's positive outlook was driven by Rocket Lab's strong execution, highlighted by a record third quarter and a major contract win from the U.S. Space Development Agency.

Additionally, Rocket Lab's recent contract from the U.S. Space Development Agency for $816 million is the largest in the company's history. The award effectively doubled Rocket Lab's total backlog, marking a major step forward for the business. Under the contract, Rocket Lab will design and manufacture 18 missile-warning, tracking, and defense satellites for low Earth orbit.

Sheppard also pointed to Rocket Lab's first Neutron launch, expected in the first half of 2026, as the single most important catalyst for the stock.

On the downside, he sees some risks for Rocket Lab, including possible Neutron delays, regulatory challenges, and supply-chain disruptions. Overall, he has strong confidence in Rocket Lab's proven launch track record and its evolution into a full-scale space infrastructure company.

What Should Investors Watch Next?

Rocket Lab completed 21 Electron launches in fiscal 2025, its highest annual total to date, bringing its successful mission count to 79. That track record makes the company the third-most active launch provider globally, second in the U.S. behind SpaceX.

Looking ahead, both investors and analysts are focused on Neutron, Rocket Lab's medium-lift, reusable launch vehicle.

Meanwhile, management expects Neutron to reach the launchpad in Q1 2026, with its first flight soon after. If successful, Neutron could significantly improve Rocket Lab's unit economics and position the company as the only credible commercial alternative to SpaceX's Falcon 9.

Is RKLB a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, RKLB stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with six Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Rocket Lab is $71.43, suggesting a potential downside of 22.19% from the current level.

RKLB shares have surged more than 250% in the last 12 months.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: TSMC Is Set to Report Q4 Earnings Today. Here Is What the Street Expects
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) or TSMC, the world's leading contract chip maker, is set to release its full fourth-quarter earnings results on Thursday, January 15. TSM stock has gained about 64% over the past year, primarily due to strong demand for advanced chips, rising orders from key clients like Apple (AAPL) and Nvidia (NVDA), and improving chip supply conditions. Wall Street analysts expect the company to report earnings of $2.90 per share, representing a 32% increase year-over-year. Meanwhile, revenues are expected to increase by about 23% from the year-ago quarter to $32.97 billion, according to data from the TipRanks Forecast page.

data.title: Cathie Wood Sells $38M in Tesla, Buys $50M in Broadcom
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made interesting portfolio moves on Wednesday, January 14, as revealed in daily fund disclosures. The trades underscore ARK's bullish stance on the semiconductor and fintech sectors and a changing view of the auto sector.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wood, known for her long-term bets on high-growth tech like electric vehicles (EVs), appears to be shifting funds from high-growth companies to those with presumably lower, but potentially more stable, amid recent market volatility.

Wood Makes a Surprise Move on Tesla

Surprisingly, Wood sold a large chunk of her favorite EV stock, Tesla (TSLA). The ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) sold 86,136 Tesla shares for $38.52 million, marking a major reduction in the holdings. Despite this sale, Tesla still holds the number one spot on the ARKK ETF, with a 10.68% weight, and $805.74 million value as of today.

Tesla stock fell 1.8% on January 14 after CEO Elon Musk's X post that read, "Tesla will stop selling FSD after February 14." The tech will only be available as a monthly subscription thereafter. 

Meanwhile, the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) sold 19,310 shares of contract chip maker Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) for $6.39 million. The ARKW fund also offloaded 126,437 shares of Unity Software (U) for $5.77 million. Other smaller sales included Kratos Defense (KTOS), Teradyne (TER), Natera (NTRA), Intuit (INTU), and Global-E Online (GLBE) through various ARK funds.

Wood Buys the Dip in Broadcom

Wood capitalized on the 4.1% dip in Broadcom (AVGO) shares, buying a total of 143,089 shares through the ARKK and ARKW funds for a massive $50.74 million. Broadcom is a leading designer of semiconductors and infrastructure software, powering AI, networking, and cloud computing. AVGO shares slumped yesterday after news that Chinese authorities told domestic firms to steer clear of around 12 U.S. and Israeli software vendors on national security grounds. This also included Broadcom's VMware software, which was acquired in a $69 billion deal.

Additionally, Wood added 56,993 shares of Klarna Group PLC (KLAR) for $1.71 million. Klarna PLC is a leading buy now, pay later (BNPL) company with an expanding international footprint, competing with Affirm (AFRM) and Afterpay. Other smaller purchases included 72,320 shares of Kodiak AI (KDK), a developer of autonomous trucking technology.

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how some of these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool:

Currently, analysts have a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on Klarna, Taiwan Semi, and Broadcom stocks, with KLAR stock offering the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: TSM Stock Near Record Highs—Is It Still a Buy Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., or TSMC, (TSM) is set to report its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings on Thursday, January 15. While the company's Q4 revenue has already topped estimates, investors will be closely focused on margins and profitability. With the stock trading near record highs, near-term upside may be more limited. Still, TSMC's bullish outlook is supported by strong demand for advanced chips used in AI data centers, high-performance computing, and next-generation smartphones. Over the past 12 months, TSM shares have risen more than 60%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, TSMC is the world's largest contract chipmaker. The company supplies advanced semiconductors to top tech giants, including Apple (AAPL) and Nvidia (NVDA).

What to Expect from TSM's Q4 Earnings

Wall Street expects TSMC to post earnings of $2.9 per share, nearly 30% higher than a year ago. Meanwhile, revenue is forecast at $32.97 billion, up from $26.88 billion in the same quarter last year.

TSMC has already shared its monthly sales last week, with fourth-quarter revenue jumping about 20% year over year to roughly NT$1.05 trillion ($33 billion), beating analyst expectations and landing near the top of its guidance range of $32.2 billion to $33.4 billion. In the full earnings report, investors will be closely focused on profitability.

Is TSM Stock Worth Buying for the Long Term?

Because leading AI chipmakers such as Nvidia, AMD (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO) outsource manufacturing to TSMC, the company benefits from the AI boom regardless of which chips dominate. This makes TSMC a more stable, lower-risk way to gain AI exposure. Its technology leadership, pricing power, and deep relationships with major customers support record revenue and profit potential, with gross margins near 60% (57.8% in the third quarter), reinforcing earnings strength.

In addition, its shares trade at a cheaper valuation than many peers. TSM's FWD P/E ratio is at 31.02, very close to the sector's average of 32.22.

Overall, analysts remain highly bullish, expecting another record fourth-quarter profit driven by massive demand for AI infrastructure—supporting the case for a potential stock buy.

TSM Stock Forecast

According to TipRanks, TSM stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on six Buys and one Hold assigned in the last three months. At $313.45, TSMC's average stock price target implies a downside of 4.18% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Citigroup CEO Signals More Job Cuts, Says "The Bar Is Raised" After Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Citigroup (C) CEO Jane Fraser told employees that tougher standards are coming and that more job cuts are likely this year, as the bank continues to change how it runs its business. Her comments came after Citigroup reported a strong fourth quarter. Adjusted earnings per share rose 35% year over year to $1.81, beating Wall Street's estimate of $1.65. Adjusted revenue came in at $21 billion, also above the consensus forecast of $20.94 billion.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a memo to staff, Fraser said effort alone is no longer enough and that employees will be judged on results. She said she expects outdated practices to fade, making way for a more disciplined bank over the next few years.

A Sharper Message to Staff

Fraser told staff to act like owners, compete harder for clients, and avoid letting opportunities slip away. The message reflects her push to improve returns at a bank that has lagged rivals for years.

Citigroup has struggled to rebuild trust with investors since the financial crisis, weighed down by weak controls and uneven results. Fraser has made fixing those problems a priority since taking over as CEO in 2021.

Technology to Change How Work Gets Done

Fraser also said automation and AI will play a bigger role across the bank. As processes become simpler, some jobs will change, new roles will appear, and others will no longer be needed.

Citigroup is already looking to eliminate about 1,000 jobs this week. The bank also said most of its work to fix regulatory issues is close to finished. Once that effort ends, some staff hired for those projects may be let go.

Is Citigroup Stock a Buy? 

Ahead of the Q4 print, analysts remained highly optimistic about Citigroup's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, Citi has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 14 Buys and three Hold ratings. The average Citigroup price target of $126.33 implies 12.38% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, C shares have gained 43.6%.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Sell Off on Surprising Inflation Print as Retail Sales Shine
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower on Wednesday as investors digested new inflation and retail sales data.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In November, the Producer Price Index (PPI) climbed 0.2% month-over-month, rising from 0.1% in October and in line with the analyst estimate of 0.2%. However, PPI rose 3% year-over-year, topping the estimate of 2.7%. The PPI tracks changes in prices received by producers for goods and services and is often viewed as a leading indicator of consumer inflation. Core PPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, was unchanged from the prior month and advanced 3% year-over-year. Analysts had forecast growth of 0.2% and 2.7%, respectively.

A strong consumer spending update wasn't enough to lift the market. In November, retail sales increased 0.6% month-over-month, above the estimate of 0.5% and marking the highest rate of growth since July. At the same time, the U.S. Department of Commerce revised October's retail sales growth to a decline of 0.1% from 0%.

November is generally a strong month for sales, as it marks the beginning of the holiday shopping season and includes Black Friday. From November 1 to December 31, online consumer spending surged 6.8% from the prior year to a record high of $257.8 billion, according to Adobe (ADBE).

Meanwhile, the Supreme Court held its second opinion day of the year, although investors expecting a decision on the landmark Trump tariff case were left empty-handed. The next earliest date that SCOTUS could announce a ruling for the case is Tuesday, although uncertainty remains as the court doesn't preannounce the rulings it will address.

Still, President Trump's tariffs haven't resulted in higher inflation, according to Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia President Anna Paulson. "We've seen progress on underlying inflation that is likely to continue and there is no evidence to date that tariff-induced price increases are leading to broader inflation," she said in prepared remarks for an economic event in Philadelphia on Wednesday. Paulson added that her base case for 2026 involves inflation cooling down to the Fed's target of 2%, a stabilizing labor market, and economic growth of 2%.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.53% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 1.07%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Is About to Report Q4 Earnings. Here Is What to Expect
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on Thursday, January 15. Shares of Bank of America (BAC), Wells Fargo (WFC), and Citigroup (C) declined on Wednesday, as their Q4 2025 results failed to impress investors. Meanwhile, GS stock has rallied about 63% over the past year, driven by the strength in the bank's investment banking and bond trading revenues.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wall Street expects Goldman Sachs to report Q4 2025 earnings per share (EPS) of $11.70, reflecting a 2.1% year-over-year decline. Revenue is estimated to rise by 3.2% to $14.32 billion. While the bank is expected to benefit from solid advisory fees from mergers and acquisitions, its Q4 results might be impacted by a decline in revenue from private banking. Also, higher compensation costs are expected to weigh on the bottom line.

Analysts' Views Ahead of Goldman Sachs' Q4 Earnings

Heading into Q4 earnings, Bank of America analyst Ebrahim Poonawala increased the price target for Goldman Sachs stock to $1,050 from $900 and reaffirmed a Buy rating. Despite cyclical and regulatory tailwinds, the 5-star analyst noted that investors are concerned about the "inherent earnings volatility" associated with the capital markets business, which accounts for about 70% of overall revenue. That said, Poonawala added that the "stickier" financing revenue has outperformed the growth in trading revenue since 2021.

Meanwhile, HSBC analyst Saul Martinez lowered his price target for Goldman Sachs stock to $604 from $608 and retained a Hold rating. The analyst raised his 2025-26 adjusted EPS estimates across his bank stocks coverage by about 1% to 7% to reflect higher net interest income (NII), investment banking fees, and share repurchase estimates.

Here's What Options Traders Anticipate

Using TipRanks' Options tool, we can see what options traders are expecting from the stock immediately after its earnings report. The expected earnings move is determined by calculating the at-the-money straddle of the options closest to expiration after the earnings announcement. If this sounds complicated, don't worry, the Options tool does this for you.

Indeed, it currently says that options traders are expecting about 3.55% move in either direction in GS stock in reaction to Q4 2025 results.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Massive Outage Impacts 180,000 Verizon (VZ) Customers in U.S.
data.description:
Verizon (VZ), the largest wireless internet provider in the U.S., said it is experiencing a massive outage that is impacting 180,000 of its customers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The telecommunications giant said that the issue involves wireless internet and data services throughout America. In a social media post, Verizon said: "Our engineers are engaged and are working to identify and solve the issue quickly."

The website DownDetector said that 179,000 Verizon customers had reported problems beginning at noon on Jan. 14. The outages are mostly occurring in major U.S. cities such as New York, Los Angeles, Chicago, and Seattle.

Verizon's Problem a Mystery

It is not immediately clear what has caused the disruption to Verizon's wireless system. The company has not said what issue its engineers are working to resolve. A smaller number of customers at rival internet provider T-Mobile (TMUS) were also experiencing issues on Jan. 14, with about 1,700 users losing internet access.

AT&T (T) has said that its network is operating normally with no reported issues. The U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC), which regulates the industry, said on social media that it is paying close attention to reports of the wireless outage at Verizon.

Is VZ Stock a Buy?

Verizon's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 14 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on four Buy and 10 Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average VZ price target of $46.42 implies 16.55% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Slip as S&P 500 (SPX) Extends Its Losing Streak
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged lower on Wednesday night as the S&P 500 (SPX) came off a second straight day of losses. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index were down a modest 0.04%, 0.06%, and 0.08%, respectively, at 9:01 p.m. EDT on January 14.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On Wednesday evening, President Donald Trump signed a proclamation placing a 25% tariff on certain semiconductor imports. The measure does not apply to chips brought in to support the expansion of the U.S. technology supply chain.

By the close of trading on Wednesday, the S&P 500 fell 0.5%, the Dow slipped about 42 points, or nearly 0.1%, and the Nasdaq Composite dropped 1%. All three major indexes closed lower for a second straight day.

Stocks finished lower as losses in large technology stocks dragged on the major indexes. Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), and Amazon (AMZN) each fell more than 2%. Oracle (ORCL) and Broadcom (AVGO) slid about 4%, while Nvidia (NVDA) dipped 1.4%.

Looking ahead to Thursday, investors will turn their attention to earnings from Goldman Sachs (GS), Morgan Stanley (MS), and BlackRock (BLK). Weekly jobless claims data is also scheduled for release.
